excerpt: The vastness of the heavens is boundless and eternal, the dwelling place of the Holy Spirits. The term 'circumference' denotes that which cannot be enclosed, and simultaneously refers to the One who encloses all things
source_location_hint: On the cosmic spheres and the divine circumference

excerpt: Three forces govern the universe: from sky to earth, from earth to sky, and from sky to earth—explicating the cosmic movements through the three letters of divine unity
source_location_hint: On the three cosmic forces and the letters of Lā ilāha illā-Allāh

excerpt: The first force is centrifugal from the Kaʿba toward the constellation; the second is centripetal from the constellation toward the Kaʿba; the third presses upon the constellation from heaven's circumference
source_location_hint: On the three cosmic forces and their directions

excerpt: The twelve divisions of the zodiacal constellation in which the star-clusters are stationed number one thousand and twenty-nine, arranged in significant patterns
source_location_hint: On the zodiacal divisions and numerical correspondences

excerpt: The twelve angels who uphold the Throne direct all things by drawing forth light from the physical world and returning it to the spiritual realm, maintaining the circulation of cosmic illumination
source_location_hint: On the celestial directors and the circulation of divine light

excerpt: The Greatest Name Allāh unfolds through letters bearing spiritual significance; the Real Name enduring beyond utterance is the First Intellect and the First Source, from which all knowledge and being emanate
source_location_hint: On the divine names and the First Intellect
